homepage.title: Stoke-on-Trent South MP: Championing Local Interests
homepage.meta_description: Meet your dedicated Labour MP for Stoke-on-Trent South, committed to serving the community and representing your voice in Parliament.
headings: h2: Stay up to date with the latest news
h2: Sign up to my newsletter
homepage.text_excerpt: Stoke-on-Trent South MP: Championing Local Interests Home Campaigns Pride in Place Longton Town of Culture About Contact News Home Campaigns Pride in Place Longton Town of Culture About Contact News More Home Campaigns Pride in Place Longton Town of Culture About Contact News Home Campaigns Pride in Place Longton Town of Culture About Contact News For the People For the People For the People For the People sign up to my newsletter About Me I settled in North Staffs to be near family, to continue my career as a teacher and lecturer in AI and am now proud to work with our NHS. I am determined to repay the warm welcome I was given as your effective, hard-working Member of Parliament. I'm local, experienced and the fresh start we need. Find out more about me Stay up to date with the latest news Sign up to my newsletter For the People of Stoke-on-Trent Email* Sign up Copyright © 2024 Allison Gardner - All Rights Reserved. pages.title: About
pages.text_excerpt: About Home Campaigns Pride in Place Longton Town of Culture About Contact News Home Campaigns Pride in Place Longton Town of Culture About Contact News More Home Campaigns Pride in Place Longton Town of Culture About Contact News Home Campaigns Pride in Place Longton Town of Culture About Contact News About me I previously worked with the NHS as a Senior Scientific Adviser for Artificial Intelligence. I also served as a director of 'Women Leading in AI' and worked internationally with governments and organizations such as the UN to advocate for the ethical use of Artificial Intelligence. Before this, I spent over 25 years as a Science and PSHE teacher in secondary schools. During that time, I completed a PhD in Molecular Biology (for fun!) and later lectured at Keele University, where I led the Data Science Degree Apprenticeship programme. I spent six years as a councillor in Newcastle-under-Lyme, where I also served as Chair of the CLP. I was the campaign coordinator for the 2017 general election, achieving a remarkable victory against all odds. Additionally, I was one of the leading campaigners to save Bradwell Community Hospital and have earned respect across North Staffordshire for my passionate commitment to the area. As a 'military baby,' I was born in Singapore, where my Navy father was stationed, but I was raised by my Irish mother, who worked as a factory manager. I credit my mum, who began her working life as a cleaner, for instilling in me a strong work ethic, resilience, and determination to succeed. My Values Championing equality and fairness: I believe we should all be treated with dignity and respect, that diversity is to be celebrated and we are all valued. Fighting for social justice: I believe in standing up for the most vulnerable and tackling poverty and injustice wherever we find it. Empowering communities and engaging people: I believe in thriving communities with the resources to work together to achieve a better quality of life for all. Defending and growing our public services: I believe in high quality, accessible services to meet the needs of our communities and our people at their heart. Hope for all our futures: I believe in the politics of hope that enables people to transform their lives and our environment for a fairer, greener and happier future. Subscribe to my newsletter Sign up to keep up to date Email Sign up Copyright © 2024 Allison Gardner - All Rights Reserved. I want to help My priority is supporting the people of Stoke-on-Trent South and working to improve the quality of life for everyone in our communities. If you need assistance, please reach out via email.
